strengths: Exceeds the 15-year minimum experience requirement with a career spanning since 2006 across diverse domains.
Demonstrates strong technical breadth, with hands-on experience in web, mobile, embedded systems, and multiple programming languages.
Shows initiative and knowledge-sharing through founding an open-source project (GISCollective) and leading technical workshops.
concerns: Lacks the required Director-level strategic experience; the resume details a career as a hands-on individual contributor (Senior/Staff Engineer) focused on implementation, not high-level strategy.

AI expertise appears superficial and very recent, limited to a current role contributing to 'AI-agent infrastructure' without evidence of designing or leading the development of AI-powered products or platforms.
No experience advising strategic customers or partners, a key responsibility of the role, indicating a potential gap in external-facing leadership and influence.
response.justification: The candidate is a skilled and versatile senior software engineer, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with the Director-level strategic nature of this role. His experience is in hands-on software craftsmanship and implementation, not defining high-level product strategy, advising enterprise customers, or demonstrating visionary leadership. His exposure to AI is nascent and tactical, falling significantly short of the 'recognized expertise' and proven track record of building AI-powered platforms required to succeed in this position.
